events.title: Pride – Nail Painting and Bracelet Making
events.description: Children are welcome to explore self-expression with nail painting and making beaded bracelets as well as ribbon butterfly hairclips! Staff will provide non-toxic nail polish for children and caregivers to apply to their fingernails. Nail polish can easily be removed by either peeling when dry or washing hands in warm water. For school-aged children and their caregivers. Drop-in. No registration.

Join Non-Binary Drag Artist Dank Sinatra for a half-hour program, featuring stories all about self-expression, self-acceptance, community togetherness, and familial love, with plenty of songs and dances the whole family can enjoy. Storytime: 4:00-4:30pm Stay & Play: 4:30-5:00pm

events.title: L'Heure du conte / French Storytime

Families are welcome to "stay and play" for an additional 30 minutes following the program. -------

events.title: The Hidden Rivers
events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Led by Shannyn Porter and Ursula Handleigh

Kids will learn the basic stitches, how to follow simple patterns, and how to use colorful threads to bring a design to life.  By the end of the program, each child will have completed a bright and cheerful stitched picture to proudly take home and display. Registering will register your child for all 4 sessions.

events.title: Botanical Photography / Sunprints
events.description: An alternative eco-friendly photography process that uses botanicals as both its subject matter and development process. This safe chemistry-free activity is adaptable for a broad group of ages Led by Ursula Handleigh

events.title: The Hidden Rivers
events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Looking at archival images and current references, participants will be guided to bring their own stories into our city’s images using material such as watercolours, crayons, playdough, text, etc onto colouring pages. Led by Shannyn Porter and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Solar System Stories: A Merril Collection Exhibit
events.description: Take a trip around our solar system without leaving the comfort of planet Earth. Explore the strange landscapes of the inner and outer planets and their moons. We've even included Pluto! Learn how our understanding of these worlds has evolved since their discovery. In our exhibit you'll find original art, novels, short stories and more set on these fantastic celestial bodies. This exhibit can be viewed during the Merril Collection's regular hours. It opens on Tuesday, May 19 following the holida

events.title: Branch Art Exhibit: When The Cavas Speaks-Artistic Journeys by Sarah Chi
events.description: Throughout the month of June, works by artist Sarah Chi are on display at Northern District Library branch in an exhibit. Sarah is her school’s Art Club Head, a TPL Teen Council and Young Voices Council member. She has created art since age six and began expressing personal ideas at fourteen. Working in oil, acrylics, ink, printmaking, and mixed media, she has won Scholastic Art & Writing Awards (Gold and Silver Key) and had work published in Young Voices magazine. She co-founded

events.title: Soccer Scavenger Hunt

events.title: Toys Alive! Exhibition Tour
events.description: Curious about what your toys get up to when you are not looking? We jumped into the toy box to discover just that. From dolly’s adventures to teddy bears on the search for honey to toy soldiers on patrol. Join us for a free guided tour of TD Gallery’s current exhibition Toys Alive! Explore literary works from the late 1800s to present day from the Osborne Collection of Early Children’s Books! Registration is not required but is encouraged. Please meet at the entrance to the TD Gallery.  Inf

Meet new folks, build connections, and tell us about programs and services you would like to see in the neighbourhood! Come join us every Saturday for screenings of your favourite movies. In addition to this, enjoy cuddles with a therapy dog! Therapy dog Goose visits Fort York Branch on alternate Saturdays from 11:00AM-12:00PM. We'll be showing the following films: June 6 - Lilo&Stitch (2025) June 13 -

events.title: Instructional Chess
events.description: The Chess Institute of Canada is bringing chess to life at your local library! Join us as we explore the ancient game of chess and the essential life-skills the game imparts. Come and take in a lesson on the great game or sit down and start playing!  Learn, compete, connect with your community through the game of chess. Open to all ages. Children under 12 need to be supervised.

events.title: Saturday Chess Club
events.description: Join us on the 2nd floor every Saturday for a few games of chess in a welcoming environment. All ages and all levels are welcome. Children must be accompanied by a parent or guardian to participate. Any unsupervised individuals under 18 will not be able to participate in the program. This is a drop-in program. Please note that there will be no chess club on Saturday May 16 for our Big Book Sale.

events.title: Family Movie: Flubber
events.description: Rated G, approx. 94min Join us on a Saturday afternoon with Professor Phillip, Flubber & Weebo as they find different ways to have fun. Don’t forget the popcorn! Family, Drop-in

events.title: Screenwriting for Beginners
events.description: Have an idea for a movie, but not sure how to write it? Writing a screenplay can be tricky as the industry has strict rules on how they are formatted.This workshop will guide participants on how to properly format a screenplay and the different software available to make this process easier. Computers will be provided for this program. events.title: Paint Day!

After that, you can start crafting a beautiful masterpiece, or just have some fun experimenting with colours! All materials will be provided. Suitable for families and people of all ages. events.title: Robotics Workshops for Youth
events.description: This program is for kids and youth in grades 3-12. Join Upper Toronto Community Robotics for accessible, hands-on robotics and engineering workshops for youth! These programs will help you build foundational skills in engineering design, safety, CAD software, 3D modeling, and introductory robotics coding. This is a drop-in program.

events.title: 3D Printing Demo - All Ages
events.description: Watch a 3D printer in action, see samples, learn how they work, and how you can use them at TPL's Digital Innovation Hubs. Note: Customers are no longer required to attend a mandatory 3D printing class prior to using a 3D printer at the Toronto Public Library’s Digital Innovation Hubs. This class is for those who are interested in learning more about 3D printing and how to use the Prusa software. Registration will start 2 weeks before this class.

events.title: Container Gardening: Supporting Our Local Pollinators in Small Spaces
events.description: Pollinators need a supply of nectar throughout the growing season, so if you are planting up pots for your garden or balcony, why not choose plants that will attract birds, bees, and butterflies?  This demonstration, led by a Toronto Master Gardener, will start with how to choose soil, plants, and containers, and how to arrange them to maximize their positive impact on the environment. Join us for one or all of our fabulous Nail Nights on the first Monday of every month. Drop-in, no registration required. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Mindful Mondays
events.description: Every Monday, enjoy a series of stress-relieving mindfulness activities, crafts, and games to help ease anxiety and give you a moment of Zen. Enjoy mindful colouring sheets, rainbow scratch art, mindfulness card decks, gratitude journals, and more. All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by

events.title: The A(i), B, C’s of Artificial Intelligence
events.description: This week-long workshop series introduces teens to the four "must knows" of Artificial Intelligence. Through video clips, props, an original AI literacy song, discussions, and hands-on activities, participants will explore how AI works, how it’s used, and how to think critically about its impact. By the end of the program, attendees will be better equipped to navigate AI and social media with awareness and creativity. The workshop is led by Keith McDonald, founder and Chief Curator of Toron

events.title: Sushi Making Workshop with Chef Sang Kim for Teens
events.description: Award-winning writer, chef, and TV personality, Sang Kim, teaches how to make vegetarian sushi making. Participants will make sushi rolls using ingredients prepared by the Chef and eat their own creations. A brief history of sushi, its origins and eating etiquette will also be shared. Limited spots available. Registration is required.

events.title: Sunday Family Game Time
events.description: Join us for a family games afternoon from Lego to Uno. We will also have some colouring and crafts for everyone Every Sunday afternoon all ages welcome

events.title: Sunday Family Club
events.description: Enjoy and have fun on Sunday afternoons at Guildwood Library! Will you colour your favourite characters, do a craft or show off your building skills with LEGO and KEVA? Maybe it will be Snap Circuits or Board Games? Every Sunday come in for a surprise!

events.title: Chess and Board Game Cafe
events.description: Join us for a relaxed session of games and good company. We’ve got chess sets and a great selection of board games ready to play. Whether you're into strategy or just here for some laughs, all skill and interest levels are welcome! Drop-in (no registration required).

events.title: Youth Art and Game Workshop
events.description: This program is part of Youth service in a welcoming place for teens in the summer.  Do you love Games and crafts? Bring your creativity and imagination to our workshop for a fun day at the library. Workshops include DIY button making, bookmark, phone charms, board games, and more! Registration is required. events.description: Award-winning writer, chef, and TV personality, Sang Kim, teaches how to make the iconic Korean kimbap, exactly like you see them on your favourite K-dramas. events.title: Toys Alive! Exhibition Tour
events.description: Curious about what your toys get up to when you are not looking? We jumped into the toy box to discover just that. Explore literary works from the late 1800s to present day from the Osborne Collection of Early Children’s Books! Registration is not required but is encouraged. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: 3D Fundamentals with Blender Part III: 3D Animation
events.description: In this part of the Blender 3D Fundamentals Series, participants will learn how to use and understand 3D animation tools such as armatures, keyframes, the dopesheet and graph editor to bring their 3D model to life. Registration is required. Computers will provided for this class. events.title: Employment Summer Workshop
events.description: The Canadian Training Institute is thrilled to present a two- day series that will introduce participants to employment and job search skills for future success Topics will include: Resume Basics Job Search Strategies Interview Skills and Preparation Volunteer hours can be provided to participants upon completion of the camp.

events.description: Learn the basics of how to make the classic Italian dessert, Tiramisu. events.title: Throwback Thursdays: Great Movies on the Big Screen
events.description: Come join us for a radical retro rewind projected on the big screen. Whether you're a first-time viewer or long-time fan, these movies are sure to please. May 28: Star Trek (2009, PG) June 4: Crazy Stupid Love (2001, PG) June 11: The Grand Budapest Hotel (2014, 14A) June 18: Best in Show (2000, PG) June 25: The Big Lebowski (1998, 14A) All are welcome. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: Boba Drink Making Workshop with Chef Yumiko for Teens
events.description: In this 45 minute to 1 hour workshop (depending on the level of engagement), participants will learn how to prepare a classic milk bubble tea, including decorating and mixing techniques, as well as the breakdown of core ingredients. A classic lidded cup and straw will be provided to enjoy the boba as you go! Food allergy notice: Please be aware that food will be used in this program. Certain food ingredients may cause allergic reactions in some people.  (*contains dairy, sugar, and caffeine

events.title: 3D Design with Autodesk Fusion
events.description: A hands-on workshop where participants will become familiar with working in a 3D environment by creating simple 3D objects using Autodesk Fusion software. events.title: Youth Hub: Hands-On Dinosaur Fossils & Ancient Life
events.description: Join us for an opportunity to handle, examine up close and discuss nine real and replica fossils in this fascinating hands-on workshop. Participants will be entertained and educated, not just about the fossils themselves, but the paleontological aspects that underpin them. Concepts include: evolution, fossilization, ‘The Tree of Life’ (phylogeny) and — of course — dinosaurs! This program is part of Youth Hub service. events.title: Saturday Chess Club
events.description: Join us on the 2nd floor every Saturday for a few games of chess in a welcoming environment. All ages and all levels are welcome. Children must be accompanied by a parent or guardian to participate. Any unsupervised individuals under 18 will not be able to participate in the program. This is a drop-in program. Please note that there will be no chess club on Saturday May 16 for our Big Book Sale.

events.title: Robotics Workshops for Youth
events.description: This program is for kids and youth in grades 3-12. Join Upper Toronto Community Robotics for accessible, hands-on robotics and engineering workshops for youth! These programs will help you build foundational skills in engineering design, safety, CAD software, 3D modeling, and introductory robotics coding. This is a drop-in program.

events.title: Screenwriting for Beginners
events.description: Have an idea for a movie, but not sure how to write it? Writing a screenplay can be tricky as the industry has strict rules on how they are formatted.This workshop will guide participants on how to properly format a screenplay and the different software available to make this process easier. Computers will be provided for this program. events.title: Botanical Photography / Sunprints
events.description: An alternative eco-friendly photography process that uses botanicals as both its subject matter and development process. I will teach participants how to make their own simple chemical-free photography developer and discuss alternative negatives. Participants will be encouraged to use bring plants from home, leaves found on site, etc to use for the process. This safe chemistry-free activity is adaptable for a broad group of ages Led by Ursula Handleigh

events.description: Workplace Readiness & Soft Skill This session focuses on essential workplace skills such as communication, teamwork, time management, and professionalism. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available.&nbs

events.title: Cercle de Conversation Française / French Conversation Circle
events.description: Nous vous invitons à pratiquer votre français dans un environnement informel ! Tous les niveaux sont bienvenus. We invite you to practice your French in an informal environment! This French Conversation Circle is open to all levels. Accessibilité: Le sous-titrage en direct ou automatique, l’interprétation en langue des signes, les services d’intervenant, ou les services d’accompagnement sont disponibles sur demande. Veuillez soumettre votre demande au moins deux semaines à l’avance à : tpl.ca/ac

events.title: LivingWorks SafeTALK
events.description: LivingWorks SafeTALK is a half-day workshop that prepares participants to recognize when someone might be having thoughts of suicide and how to connect them to life-saving support. Learn practical skills to help someone keep safe and guide them toward further help.  Who should attend? Individuals aged 15 and up People who want to support suicide safety in their community No previous training or experience is required. All participants will receive a LivingWorks SafeTALK certificat

events.title: Disassemble a Desktop Computer
events.description: Have you ever wondered what's going on inside a computer? Ever wanted to take apart a computer to see? This workshop is your chance! Free Geek Toronto is a non-profit organization dedicated to getting unwanted computers and electronics back into working condition. During this workshop, we'll go over what's inside a computer, how to properly open a desktop, and safety guidelines for working with electronics.

Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Seniors E-Connect
events.description: Are you 55+? Do you have questions about your tablet, phone, laptop, email or an app? Do you need help with eBooks or other online library services? Bring in your own device and our teen volunteers are here to help!

events.title: Robotics Workshops for Youth
events.description: This program is for kids and youth in grades 3-12. Join Upper Toronto Community Robotics for accessible, hands-on robotics and engineering workshops for youth! These programs will help you build foundational skills in engineering design, safety, CAD software, 3D modeling, and introductory robotics coding. This is a drop-in program.

events.title: Common Misconceptions About Investing
events.description: Presented by Riverdale Immigrant Women’s Centre (RIWC) , this workshop helps to address myths about investing and introduces simple, beginner-friendly investment concepts only. Registration encouraged. Drop-ins are welcome until capacity is reached.
